title: Assignment to secondary linux user group differs from building to running stage
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The secondary group membership of a linux user while building the image is different to running commands in the container. In other words if you add a user to a secondary group in a Dockerfile it takes effect but only during the "building stage" of the docker image. If you later run a command in the container itself (e.g. within an entrypoint script or CMD) the assigned secondary group is gone.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Paste the flowing content to a file named Dockerfile and save it into a new folder called testing

   ```dockerfile
   FROM ubuntu:18.04

   # myuser: member of ssl-cert group (read access to TLS.key)
   RUN addgroup --gid 117 ssl-cert &&\
       adduser --gecos "" --disabled-password --uid 1000 --shell /bin/bash myuser &&\
       adduser myuser ssl-cert

   # untrusted: NOT a member of ssl-cert group (NO read access to TLS.key)
   RUN adduser --gecos "" --disabled-password --uid 1001 --shell /bin/bash --no-create-home untrusted

   # create TLS.key (read only for ssl-cert; no access to others)
   RUN echo "I can read the TLS.key file." > TLS.key &&\
       chown root:ssl-cert TLS.key &&\ 
       chmod 640 TLS.key

   # switch to myuser 
   USER myuser

   # check that myuser is member of ssl-cert group: SUCCESS 
   RUN groups $USER &&\
       cat /etc/group | grep ssl-cert &&\
       cat TLS.key

   # check that myuser is member of ssl-cert group: FAIL
   CMD groups $USER &&\
       cat /etc/group | grep ssl-cert &&\
       cat TLS.key

   ```

2. Build the image with `podman build -t testing .` from within the testing folder.

3. Run a container from that image: `podman run -it --rm testing`

4. Observe the difference between the output of STEP 6 during build and the output of running the container. Note that both commands are exactly the same.

**Describe the results you received:**

myuser can read TLS.key only during image build time not while running the container although he is a member of ssl-cert.

```shell
$ podman build -t testing .
[...]
STEP 6: RUN groups $USER &&    cat /etc/group | grep ssl-cert &&    cat TLS.key
myuser ssl-cert
ssl-cert:x:117:myuser
I can read the TLS.key file.
STEP 7: CMD groups $USER &&    cat /etc/group | grep ssl-cert &&    cat TLS.key
STEP 8: COMMIT testing

$ podman run -it --rm testing
myuser
ssl-cert:x:117:myuser
cat: TLS.key: Permission denied
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

myuser should be in ssl-cert group also while running the container.

```shell
$ podman build -t testing .
[...]
STEP 6: RUN groups $USER &&    cat /etc/group | grep ssl-cert &&    cat TLS.key
myuser ssl-cert
ssl-cert:x:117:myuser
I can read the TLS.key file.
STEP 7: CMD groups $USER &&    cat /etc/group | grep ssl-cert &&    cat TLS.key
STEP 8: COMMIT testing

$ podman run -it --rm testing
myuser
ssl-cert:x:117:myuser
I can read the TLS.key file.
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This behaviour is specific to podman. The 'Describe the results you expected' section is a straight copy from docker.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14.9
Built:        Wed Sep 30 21:31:11 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
$ podman info --debug

  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: b66ffa8e262507e37fca689822d23430f3357fe8
      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2
  swapFree: 2147479552
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-2.1.1-7.fc32.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No, but I mailed this issue to the security list and @rhatdan proposed to open an issue here.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 32

comments.body: Initial guess is that Podman is not joining supplemental groups for that container - seems like a bug. I'll take a look after lunch.
I can replicate with Podman v2.1.1, but not with v2.2.0 (just released yesterday). So I believe we fixed this one at some point. Can you grab the new v2.2.0 release and re-verify?

I confirm this issue is not present in podman v2.2.1 (at least on the build provided by Fedora 33).
Thanks.
